data.title: Exxon Mobil Algae Setback Puts Integrity And Future Investments Under Review
data.description:
• Exxon Mobil (NYSE:XOM) reportedly continued to promote its $500 million algae biofuel project after internal findings in 2020 highlighted major commercial and technical hurdles.
  • The algae program remained a core part of the company’s public low carbon messaging for several years before eventually being canceled.
  • This gap between public promotion and internal assessments raises fresh questions about transparency and execution risk around Exxon Mobil’s future facing investments.

For investors following NYSE:XOM, this story sits at the intersection of traditional oil and gas operations and the company’s push into lower carbon initiatives. The algae project had been positioned as a high profile example of potential next generation fuels. The new information instead steers attention back to how Exxon Mobil evaluates, governs, and communicates early stage technologies inside a large, established energy business.

Looking ahead, the key question is how Exxon Mobil will structure and disclose future alternative energy programs so investors can better judge their robustness. Scrutiny around project selection, internal checkpoints, and the alignment of marketing with technical data may become more central to how the market assesses management quality and long term capital allocation.

data.title: C Link Squared FY 2025 Loss Of MYR165 Million Challenges Bullish Narratives
data.description:
C-Link Squared (SEHK:1463) has released its FY 2025 numbers with first half revenue of MYR55.5 million and a basic EPS loss of MYR0.0575 per share, setting a cautious tone for investors watching how the business is handling profitability pressure. Over the last three reported halves, revenue has moved from MYR49.6 million in 1H FY 2024 to MYR47.4 million in 2H FY 2024 and then to MYR55.5 million in 1H FY 2025. Over the same periods, net income has swung from a profit of MYR1.1 million to losses of MYR22.5 million and MYR165.3 million, signalling margins that remain firmly under strain.

See our full analysis for C-Link Squared.

With the headline numbers set, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the prevailing market narratives around C-Link Squared and where those stories might be challenged by the latest margin picture.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

Losses Deepen To MYR182.1 Million On A TTM Basis

  • Over the last twelve months, C-Link Squared reported total revenue of MYR126.6 million and a net loss of MYR182.1 million, so the business is currently generating more in losses than in sales.
  • Critics highlight a bearish pattern of worsening profitability, and the trailing loss of MYR182.1 million alongside a five year profit decline of 88.1% a year strongly supports that concern.
    • On a half year view, net income moved from a loss of MYR22.5 million in 2H FY 2024 to a loss of MYR165.3 million in 1H FY 2025, which points to heavier pressure on margins rather than stabilisation.
    • Basic EPS over the latest twelve month periods shifted from a small loss of MYR0.00754 per share to a loss of MYR0.9641 per share, which lines up with the view that earnings quality is weak at present.

Revenue Holds Above MYR100 Million While Margins Stay Under Strain

  • The trailing twelve month revenue stepped from MYR96.99 million to MYR102.9 million and then to MYR126.6 million, yet net losses over those same snapshots widened from MYR21.4 million to MYR187.9 million and then MYR182.1 million.
  • What stands out against a more bullish narrative about business scale is that higher revenue has not translated into profit, because losses have remained large compared with the MYR126.6 million of sales.
    • At the half year level, 1H FY 2025 revenue of MYR55.5 million sits above 1H FY 2024 revenue of MYR49.6 million, while net income swung from a profit of MYR1.1 million to a loss of MYR165.3 million, so margin compression is the key issue rather than top line traction.
    • Basic EPS followed the same pattern, turning from a small profit of MYR0.000397 per share in 1H FY 2024 to a loss of MYR0.0575 per share in 1H FY 2025, which challenges any bullish view that scale alone is enough to improve per share outcomes.

P/S Of 3.6x Sits Between Industry And Peers

  • C-Link Squared trades on a P/S of 3.6x, which is higher than the wider Asian Professional Services industry average of 1.4x but below the peer group average of 4.6x.
  • Consensus style commentary often frames this as a mixed valuation signal, and the combination of a premium to the industry alongside trailing twelve month losses of MYR182.1 million and share price volatility means investors are paying more than the sector average while earnings remain in the red.
    • The current share price of HK$0.305, when set against unprofitable trailing twelve month earnings and an 88.1% annual decline in profits over five years, suggests the market is still assigning value despite the loss making record.
    • At the same time, sitting below the 4.6x peer P/S leaves room for investors to debate whether the 3.6x multiple reflects the company’s multi line business mix or ongoing concern about the scale of the MYR182.1 million trailing loss.

With no clear bull or bear community narratives flagged for this name, it can help to see how different investors are interpreting the same set of numbers in real time through community discussion 📊 Read the what the Community is saying about C-Link Squared.

For investors watching Exelon at a share price of $49.02, The Exelon Promise adds a fresh angle to the story around regulated utilities and consumer affordability. At the same time, this type of customer-first initiative has to be funded, either through internal cost controls, rate design or external support, so investors will be watching how Exelon balances its already stretched financial profile, including flagged concerns around interest coverage and leverage, with the cost of these commitments.

data.title: Is It Time To Reassess Bavarian Nordic (CPSE:BAVA) After Vaccine Contract Headlines?
data.description:
• Investors may be wondering whether Bavarian Nordic is attractively priced at its current level, or if the recent run has already incorporated most of the value.
  • The stock last closed at kr197.25, with returns of 3.8% over 7 days, 2.4% over 30 days, 2.3% year to date, 30.7% over 1 year, 1.0% over 3 years, and a 31.8% decline over 5 years.
  • Recent news coverage has focused on Bavarian Nordic's role in vaccines and biopharmaceuticals. This has kept attention on the company as investors reassess sector risk. Headlines around the product portfolio, regulatory milestones, and contract pipelines have helped frame the share price moves in the context of long term demand expectations.
  • Bavarian Nordic currently has a valuation score of 6 out of 6. The sections that follow break this down across different valuation methods and also point to a deeper way of thinking about value at the end of the article.

Bavarian Nordic delivered 30.7% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Biotechs industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Bavarian Nordic Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes projected future cash flows and then discounts them back to what they might be worth today. It is essentially asking what an investor might reasonably pay now for all the cash the company is estimated to generate in future.

Bavarian Nordic’s latest twelve month free cash flow is DKK 1,400.95m. Using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model, analysts have provided explicit projections out to 2030, with free cash flow for that year estimated at DKK 1,785m. Beyond the initial analyst horizon, Simply Wall St extrapolates cash flows, which are then discounted to reflect risk and the time value of money.

On this basis, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of DKK 486.06 per share. Compared with the recent share price of DKK 197.25, the DCF output suggests the shares trade at a 59.4% discount to that intrinsic estimate. This indicates that Bavarian Nordic appears undervalued on this cash flow view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Bavarian Nordic is undervalued by 59.4%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 253 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Bavarian Nordic.

Approach 2: Bavarian Nordic Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about what you are paying for each unit of current earnings. It quickly links the share price to the business’s ability to generate profit, which many investors treat as a primary anchor for value.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth potential and risk profile. Higher growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher risk usually leads to a lower figure.

Bavarian Nordic currently trades on a P/E of 11.09x. This is below the Biotechs industry average P/E of 25.12x and also below the peer group average of 17.87x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Bavarian Nordic is 11.81x. This proprietary metric estimates a P/E that may be appropriate for the company given factors such as its earnings growth, industry, profit margins, market cap and identified risks.

Because the Fair Ratio is tailored to the company’s specific profile, it can be more informative than a simple comparison with broad industry or peer averages. Bavarian Nordic’s actual P/E of 11.09x is slightly below the Fair Ratio of 11.81x. This suggests that the shares may appear undervalued on this multiple-based view.

Result: UNDERVALUED
